request.question: The actor that played "Stiles" Stilinski in the series "Teen Wolf", co-starred with Mark Wahlberg and Kurt Russell in what 2016 film?

title: Mythology of Teen Wolf
Linden Ashby
Teen Wolf (2011 TV series)
Elvis (1979 film)
Dylan O'Brien
Teen Wolf (season 4)
Deepwater Horizon (film)
Teen Wolf (1986 TV series)
List of Teen Wolf characters
Baltasar Kormákur
sentences: The mythology of the MTV supernatural action drama series "Teen Wolf" follows a teenager named Scott McCall (Tyler Posey), who is bitten by an Alpha werewolf and must cope with how it affects his life and the lives of those closest to him and Dylan O'Brien as "Stiles" Stilinski, Scott's best friend.

Most mythological elements in Teen Wolf relate to supernatural creatures and draws heavily from Greek mythology, Norse mythology, Japanese mythology, Aztec mythology, Native American beliefs and Celtic herbalism.
The show has created its own unique mythos centered around shapeshifters and their connections to one-another throughout the world.
Clarence Linden Garnett Ashby III (born May 23, 1960) is an American actor.
He is known for his roles as Johnny Cage in the film adaptation of "Mortal Kombat", and as Dr. Brett Cooper on "Melrose Place".
He is also known for his role as Sheriff Noah Stilinski in the MTV series "Teen Wolf" (2011-2017).
Teen Wolf is an American television series developed by Jeff Davis for MTV.

It is loosely based on the 1985 film of the same name, and stars Tyler Posey as a teenager named Scott McCall, who is bitten by a werewolf and must cope with how it affects his life and the lives of those closest to him, and Dylan O'Brien as "Stiles" Stilinski, Scott's best friend.
The series has received generally positive reviews from critics and is a fan favorite on social media.
Elvis is a 1979 American made-for-television biographical film directed by John Carpenter, and starring Kurt Russell as Elvis Presley, originally aired on ABC.
It marks the last role on television for Russell, and the first collaboration between him and Carpenter.
Kurt Russell's real-life wife and father, Season Hubley and Bing Russell, co-star as Presley's own wife and father.
Dylan O'Brien (born August 26, 1991) is an American actor.

He first received attention as "Stiles" Stilinski in the MTV series "Teen Wolf", and transitioned to film in projects such as "The First Time", "The Internship" and "Deepwater Horizon", and as the lead in the dystopian science-fiction adventure series "The Maze Runner", consisting of "The Maze Runner" and its sequel, "".
He is set to reprise his role in the final installment, ""; its release, which is scheduled for January 26, 2018, was delayed after O'Brien was injured in an on-set accident.
The fourth season of "Teen Wolf", an American supernatural drama created by Jeff Davis and to some extent, based on the Teen Wolf 1985 film of the same name, premiered on June 23, 2014.
The series was renewed for a fourth season of 12 episodes on October 12, 2013.
Deepwater Horizon is a 2016 American disaster film based on actual events.
It was directed by Peter Berg from a screenplay by Matthew Michael Carnahan and Matthew Sand.
It stars Mark Wahlberg, Kurt Russell, John Malkovich, Gina Rodriguez, Dylan O'Brien and Kate Hudson.
It is based on a 2010 article by David Barstow, David Rohde and Stephanie Saul on the "Deepwater Horizon" explosion and oil spill in the Gulf of Mexico.

Teen Wolf, known as The Cartoon Adventures of Teen Wolf in the United Kingdom, is an animated American television series broadcast from 1986 to 1987 that was produced by Southern Star Productions and Hanna-Barbera Australia in association with Clubhouse Pictures in the first season and Atlantic/Kushner-Locke in the second season.
It was based on the 1985 live-action film, "Teen Wolf".
"Teen Wolf" is an American television series that airs on MTV.
The series premiered on Sunday, June 5, 2011, following the 2011 MTV Movie Awards.
"Teen Wolf" is a supernatural drama series that follows Scott McCall (Tyler Posey), a high school student and social outcast who is bitten by a werewolf.
He tries to maintain a normal life while hiding his secret and dealing with supernatural dangers that plague the town of Beacon Hills.
He is aided by his best friend, Stiles Stilinski (Dylan O'Brien), and mysterious werewolf, Derek Hale (Tyler Hoechlin).
Baltasar Kormákur Samper (born 27 February 1966) is an Icelandic actor, theater and film director, and film producer.

He is best known for directing the films "101 Reykjavík", "Hafið", "A Little Trip to Heaven" (starring Julia Stiles and Forest Whitaker), a film based on the book "Mýrin" "(Jar City)" by Arnaldur Indriðason, "Contraband", "2 Guns" (starring Mark Wahlberg and Denzel Washington) and "Everest".
His father is the Spanish painter Baltasar Samper and actor Baltasar Breki Samper is his son.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.summary_1:
Primary topics: Dylan O'Brien; the 2016 film "Deepwater Horizon"

- "Dylan O'Brien" — "first received attention as 'Stiles' Stilinski" in the MTV series (passage 2).  
- "Teen Wolf" — identifies Dylan O'Brien as "'Stiles' Stilinski" (passage 1).  
- "'Deepwater Horizon' is a 2016 American disaster film" and it "stars Mark Wahlberg, Kurt Russell, ... Dylan O'Brien" (passage 5).  
- Ambiguity/missing facts: none for answering the question — the passages link Dylan O'Brien (Stiles) to the 2016 film; "no additional retrieval needed" to determine the film title.  
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives:
  1. Deepwater Horizon (film) — lede/infobox and "Cast" section (confirm casting and year).  
  2. Dylan O'Brien — lede/filmography section (confirm film credit).  
  3. Teen Wolf (2011 TV series) — lede/cast (confirm role name).
response.hop2_query: Deepwater Horizon cast Mark Wahlberg Kurt Russell Dylan O Brien
hop2_docs: Deepwater Horizon (film) | Deepwater Horizon is a 2016 American disaster film based on actual events.  It was directed by Peter Berg from a screenplay by Matthew Michael Carnahan and Matthew Sand.  It stars Mark Wahlberg, Kurt Russell, John Malkovich, Gina Rodriguez, Dylan O'Brien and Kate Hudson.  It is based on a 2010 article by David Barstow, David Rohde and Stephanie Saul on the "Deepwater Horizon" explosion and oil spill in the Gulf of Mexico.

Dylan O'Brien | Dylan O'Brien (born August 26, 1991) is an American actor.  He first received attention as "Stiles" Stilinski in the MTV series "Teen Wolf", and transitioned to film in projects such as "The First Time", "The Internship" and "Deepwater Horizon", and as the lead in the dystopian science-fiction adventure series "The Maze Runner", consisting of "The Maze Runner" and its sequel, "".  He is set to reprise his role in the final installment, ""; its release, which is scheduled for January 26, 2018, was delayed after O'Brien was injured in an on-set accident.

Mark Wahlberg | Mark Robert Michael Wahlberg (born June 5, 1971) is an American actor, producer, businessman, former model, and former rapper.  Wahlberg was known as Marky Mark in his early career as frontman for the group Marky Mark and the Funky Bunch, releasing the albums "Music for the People" and "You Gotta Believe".  Wahlberg later transitioned to acting, appearing in films such as the drama "Boogie Nights" and the satirical war comedy-drama "Three Kings" during the 1990s.  In the 2000s, he starred in the biographical disaster drama "The Perfect Storm", the science fiction film "Planet of the Apes", the heist film "The Italian Job", and the Martin Scorsese-directed neo-noir crime drama "The Departed", for which he received an Academy Award nomination for Best Supporting Actor.  In the 2010s, he starred in the action comedy "The Other Guys" alongside Will Ferrell, the biographical sports drama "The Fighter" (for which he earned an Academy Award nomination as a producer for Best Picture), the comedy "Ted", the war film "Lone Survivor", the crime comedy "Pain & Gain", the science fiction action film "" and the sequel "", the comedy "Daddy's Home", the disaster film "Deepwater Horizon", and the thriller "Patriots Day".

Efforts to stem the Deepwater Horizon oil spill | Efforts to stem the "Deepwater Horizon" oil spill were ongoing from the time that the "Deepwater Horizon" exploded on April 20, 2010 until the well was sealed by a cap on July 15, 2010.  Various species of dolphins and other mammals (61 casualties), birds (2,095 casualties), and the endangered sea turtles (467 casualties) have been killed either directly or indirectly by the oil spill.  The "Deepwater Horizon" spill has surpassed in volume the 1989 "Exxon Valdez" oil spill as the largest ever to originate in U.S.-controlled waters; it is comparable to the 1979 Ixtoc I oil spill in total volume released (Ixtoc discharged 140 e6USgal to 148 e6USgal ; as of mid-July 2010, Deepwater Horizon has spilled 90 e6USgal to 180 e6USgal ).
Broken City (disambiguation) | Broken City is a 2013 American crime film starring Mark Wahlberg and Russell Crowe.
Health consequences of the Deepwater Horizon oil spill | Health consequences of the Deepwater Horizon oil spill
Economic effects of the Deepwater Horizon oil spill | Economic effects of the Deepwater Horizon oil spill
hop2_titles: Efforts to stem the Deepwater Horizon oil spill
Health consequences of the Deepwater Horizon oil spill
Economic effects of the Deepwater Horizon oil spill
response.summary_2: 'Deepwater Horizon' is a 2016 American disaster film. Dylan O'Brien, an American actor who first received attention as "Stiles" Stilinski on Teen Wolf, appears in Deepwater Horizon alongside Mark Wahlberg and Kurt Russell.
